disease.name: Non-Alcoholic Fatty Liver Disease (NAFLD/NASH)
disease.note: Hepatic manifestation of metabolic syndrome. Spectrum from simple steatosis (NAFL) through steatohepatitis (NASH) to cirrhosis. Leading cause of chronic liver disease worldwide. Rebranded as MAFLD (Metabolic dysfunction-Associated Fatty Liver Disease).

severityAssessment.severityLabel: NASH cirrhosis decompensation -> EMERGENCY; F3-F4 fibrosis -> URGENT management; F0-F2 -> OUTPATIENT lifestyle intervention

subtypes.name: Non-Alcoholic Fatty Liver (NAFL)
subtypes.note: Steatosis in >5% of hepatocytes without ballooning or inflammation. Generally non-progressive or slowly progressive. Low risk of cirrhosis.

subtypes.name: NASH without significant fibrosis (F0-F2)
subtypes.note: Steatosis + lobular inflammation + hepatocyte ballooning. Active steatohepatitis with potential for fibrosis progression. Intervention window.

subtypes.name: NASH with advanced fibrosis (F3) or compensated cirrhosis (F4)
subtypes.note: Bridging fibrosis or cirrhosis. High risk of decompensation and HCC. Intensive management and surveillance required.

subtypes.name: NASH-related Cirrhosis (inactive, 'burnt-out' NASH)
subtypes.note: End-stage NASH where features of steatohepatitis may regress. Often labeled cryptogenic cirrhosis. Persistent risk of HCC even without steatosis.

bedside.name: Waist circumference measurement
bedside.indication: central obesity assessment, metabolic syndrome criterion

laboratory.name: LFTs (ALT, AST, GGT, ALP, bilirubin)
laboratory.indication: ALT typically > AST in early NAFLD (ratio reverses with fibrosis); GGT elevated

laboratory.name: Serum albumin
laboratory.indication: synthetic function decreases with advanced disease

laboratory.name: INR
laboratory.indication: synthetic function; prolonged in advanced fibrosis/cirrhosis

laboratory.name: Serum ferritin
laboratory.indication: often elevated in NASH (not iron overload); >1.5x ULN suggests advanced fibrosis

laboratory.name: FIB-4 score [(Age x AST) / (Platelets x sqrt(ALT))]
laboratory.indication: non-invasive fibrosis score; <1.3 excludes advanced fibrosis; >2.67 suggests advanced fibrosis

laboratory.name: NAFLD Fibrosis Score (NFS)
laboratory.indication: validated score using age, BMI, IFG/diabetes, AST/ALT ratio, albumin, platelets

laboratory.name: Enhanced Liver Fibrosis (ELF) panel
laboratory.indication: Hypertension, PIIINP, TIMP1; proprietary advanced fibrosis marker

laboratory.name: Anti-mitochondrial antibody, ANA, anti-smooth muscle
laboratory.indication: rule out autoimmune/PBC if clinical suspicion

imaging.name: Abdominal ultrasound
imaging.indication: detect steatosis (bright liver echo), hepatomegaly, splenomegaly; HCC screening if advanced

imaging.name: Transient elastography (FibroScan) with CAP (Controlled Attenuation Parameter)
imaging.indication: quantify steatosis (CAP) and fibrosis (LSM); primary non-invasive test for NAFLD

imaging.name: MRI-PDFF (Proton Density Fat Fraction)
imaging.indication: most accurate non-invasive steatosis quantification; research/clinical trial use

imaging.name: MR elastography
imaging.indication: accurate fibrosis assessment; alternatives to FibroScan

imaging.name: CT abdomen (non-contrast)
imaging.indication: liver density <40 HU suggests steatosis; radiation exposure limits repeated use

imaging.name: Liver biopsy
imaging.indication: gold standard for NASH diagnosis (steatosis + ballooning + inflammation); reserved for atypical cases, discordant non-invasive tests, or clinical trials. Histologic activity scoring via SAF or NAS.

management.protocolReference: AASLD Practice Guidelines: NAFLD/NASH; EASL-EASD-EASO Clinical Practice Guidelines: NAFLD

pathways.criteria: Steatosis on imaging or biopsy, no ballooning, minimal/no fibrosis, FIB-4 <1.3
pathways.treatment: Lifestyle modification: weight loss 7-10% of body weight (optimal). Mediterranean diet (reduce fructose, saturated fats, processed foods). Aerobic exercise ≥150 min/week + resistance training. Treat metabolic syndrome components (optimize glycemic control, lipid management, blood pressure). Metformin if diabetes (no direct liver benefit from metformin alone). Vitamin E 800 IU/day not routinely indicated (only biopsy-proven NASH without diabetes). Reassess with LFTs and FIB-4 in 1-2 years.

pathways.criteria: Biopsy-proven NASH or high clinical suspicion, stage F0-F2 fibrosis, FIB-4 1.3-2.67
pathways.treatment: Intensive lifestyle intervention (target weight loss >7-10%). Vitamin E 800 IU PO daily if non-diabetic with biopsy-proven NASH. Pioglitazone 30-45mg PO daily if diabetic or non-diabetic with NASH (benefits vs weight gain, edema, fractures). GLP-1 receptor agonists (semaglutide, liraglutide) improve steatohepatitis in clinical trials; consider if diabetic + NASH. Monitor ALT and FIB-4 q6-12m. Repeat FibroScan q2-3 years.

pathways.criteria: FIB-4 >2.67, NFS >0.676, FibroScan LSM >12 kPa, F3-F4 on biopsy or elastography
pathways.treatment: Intensive metabolic optimization. Vitamin E 800 IU/day (non-diabetic, biopsy-proven NASH). Pioglitazone 30mg/day (if diabetic/biopsy NASH). Consider obeticholic acid 10-25mg/day (OCA, FALCON trial data; FDA approved for NASH with fibrosis in some regions; monitor pruritus). Consider resmetirom (Rezdiffra) 80-100mg PO daily if F2-F3 (FDA approved 2024). HCC surveillance US + AFP q6m. Variceal screening EGD. Manage decompensation risk. Monitor for liver-related outcomes. Transplant evaluation if further progression.

pathways.criteria: Child-Pugh B/C, ascites, variceal bleed, encephalopathy, jaundice
pathways.treatment: Decompensation management (diuretics, lactulose, rifaximin, variceal banding) as per cirrhosis pathway. Transplant evaluation. DAAs if HCV present. Limit vitamin E (not proven in decompensated). Avoid obeticholic acid in decompensated (worsens liver function). Address metabolic comorbidities. Nutrition assessment and support. Weight management post-transplant.

disease.emergencyPriority: low for simple steatosis; medium for NASH F3-F4; high for decompensated
